concept_overview.title: What is Basic String Functions?
concept_overview.bodyMarkdown:
Basic String Functions focuses on this idea: Using UPPER, LOWER, and TRIM.

A good starting point is to say the requirement in plain language before writing SQL.

### Learning goals
- clean up text data with string functions
- Practice the core idea of Basic String Functions

goal_one.title: clean up text data with string functions
goal_one.bodyMarkdown:
clean up text data with string functions.

When practicing Basic String Functions, start with a focused example and verify the result carefully.

goal_two.title: Practice the core idea of Basic String Functions
goal_two.bodyMarkdown:

practice_note.title: How to practice Basic String Functions
practice_note.bodyMarkdown:
Read the requirement, predict the result, run the query, and verify whether the output really answers the question.

That habit is especially important for Basic String Functions.

module4_basic_string_functions_practice_.title: Practice: Basic String Functions
module4_basic_string_functions_practice_.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `name` and `price` columns for rows whose `price` is greater than `50`.

Then run it.
module4_basic_string_functions_practice_.hint: Use WHERE with a comparison operator.
module4_basic_string_functions_practice_.starterCode:
-- Filter rows by price
SELECT name, price
FROM products
WHERE price > 50

module4_basic_string_functions_core_conc.title: Core idea: Basic String Functions
module4_basic_string_functions_core_conc.prompt: What is the best description of Basic String Functions?

help.hint_1: Look for the option that matches the main concept directly.
help.hint_2: Ignore answers that describe unrelated SQL behavior.

module4_basic_string_functions_core_conc.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It keeps only the rows that match a condition.

module4_basic_string_functions_true_stat.title: True statements about Basic String Functions
module4_basic_string_functions_true_stat.prompt: Select all true statements about Basic String Functions.

help.hint_1: Pick statements that align with the topic summary and objectives.
help.hint_2: Reject statements that clearly describe unrelated SQL ideas.

module4_basic_string_functions_true_stat.hint: Use the summary and objectives as your guide.

options.a: Filtering is often done with WHERE.
options.b: Comparison operators help define the row condition.
options.c: Filtering always changes the stored table data.
options.d: AND and OR can change which rows stay in the output.
options.e: Filtering is unrelated to business questions.

module4_basic_string_functions_workflow_.title: Workflow for Basic String Functions
module4_basic_string_functions_workflow_.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for Basic String Functions.

help.concept: Identify which rows should stay, write the condition, then verify the result.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: decide which rows should stay in the result
tokens.t2: write the condition in the WHERE clause
tokens.t3: run the query and verify the remaining rows

module4_basic_string_functions_keyword_f.title: Filtering keyword
module4_basic_string_functions_keyword_f.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best SQL keyword.

help.hint_1: Think about which SQL term best completes the sentence.
help.hint_2: Use the topic meaning to eliminate unrelated options.

module4_basic_string_functions_keyword_f.hint: Match the sentence to the right SQL term.
